tldr: Swap a self-hosted polyglot flag backend for a framework-native Next.js/SvelteKit SDK that delegates evaluation to a provider you choose.
why_switch: Your product is consolidating on Next.js or SvelteKit and you want first-class server/edge integration
You'd rather not operate the GO Feature Flag relay proxy yourself
You want clean A/B testing tied to Vercel deployment previews
You plan to plug into a managed provider (LaunchDarkly, Statsig, ConfigCat, etc.) via official adapters
You need edge-compatible evaluation without latency penalties
what_to_export: User attribute schemas used in rules
Audit log of recent flag changes
export_method: Pull the GO Feature Flag config file from its retriever (Git, S3, or HTTP). Treat it as the canonical export and translate each flag into the schema of the provider you'll back Flags SDK with. The Flags SDK itself does not store flags; it is a framework adapter layer. Consult the official GO Feature Flag and target-provider docs for current export formats.
import_method: Pick a provider compatible with Flags SDK (e.g., LaunchDarkly, Statsig, Optimizely, Hypertune, ConfigCat, DevCycle, or Flipt — verify supported adapters in the official Flags SDK docs). Create flags in that provider, install the Flags SDK package and the matching provider adapter in your Next.js or SvelteKit app, define each flag with the SDK's flag() helper, and reference them from server components, route handlers, or middleware. Test in deployment previews before enabling in production.

concept_mapping.note: Flags SDK is a framework layer, not a backend
Keys live in code; rules live in the provider
Flags SDK is Next.js/SvelteKit-native, not OpenFeature-native
Provider SDKs talk directly from server/edge
Other languages must keep using GO Feature Flag or another solution
Self-hostable Flipt is one option if you want to stay self-hosted

gotchas: Flags SDK only covers Next.js and SvelteKit — non-JS services need their own flag client
You still need to choose and configure a provider; the SDK alone won't evaluate flags
Costs may appear if you pick a paid SaaS provider — model usage before committing
Edge runtime constraints differ from Node — verify your provider adapter supports the runtime you target
Some adapters lag upstream feature parity; check current support in official docs
Best DX is on Vercel hosting — non-Vercel deploys may lose preview-based experimentation niceties

email.subject: Migrating from GO Feature Flag to Flags SDK
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move feature flagging on the Next.js app to Vercel's Flags SDK. It's framework-native, edge-compatible, and lets us pick a provider behind it (LaunchDarkly/Statsig/ConfigCat/Flipt/etc.).

Plan:
1. Decide on a provider — managed if we want polish, Flipt if we want to stay self-hosted.
2. Export the current GO Feature Flag YAML, translate flags into the provider's schema.
3. Install Flags SDK + adapter, define flags via flag() in code, wire to server/edge handlers.
4. Validate in deployment previews, then cut over.

Note: non-JS services keep their existing flag client. Effort: 1-4 days, mostly waiting on provider config.
